api.name: required
api.description: Synced from required() validators by the Signal Forms [field] directive.

api.name: ariaDescribedBy
api.description: Id(s) of external element(s) describing this control — typically your app-rendered error message — exposed as aria-describedby.

api.name: indeterminate
api.description: Mixed state for "select all"-style parent checkboxes. Cleared automatically on the next user interaction, matching native behavior.

api.name: show
api.description: Two-way bindable open state: [(show)].

api.name: ariaLabel
api.description: Accessible name for dialogs without a DialogHeader. When a DialogHeader is present it labels the dialog automatically via aria-labelledby.

api.name: initialFocus
api.description: CSS selector for the element to focus when the dialog opens. Defaults to the browser's native behavior (first focusable element).

api.name: showing
api.description: Emits true once opened and false once the closing animation finishes.

examples.code:

<Center height="200px">

</Center>
<!-- args:
{
    orientation: 'horizontal',
    border: 'primary',
  }
-->

api.name: ariaHasPopup
api.description: Value for aria-haspopup on the trigger, describing what the popover contains (e.g. 'menu' for Menu, 'dialog' for rich content). When unset, only aria-expanded/aria-controls are managed.

examples.title: Primary
examples.code:
<!-- Storybook story: Primary -->
<Card>
<CardHeader title="Expandable Card"><ExpandToggle #toggle /></CardHeader>
<Expand [collapsed]="toggle.collapsed()">
  <CardContent>
    <Text>${ngContent}</Text>
  </CardContent>
</Expand>
</Card>
<!-- args:
{
    collapsed: false,
    ngContent: 'Expandable Content',
  }
-->

examples.title: Primary
examples.code:
<!-- Storybook story: Primary -->
<ExpandArea title="${title}" padding="${padding}" initCollapsed="${initCollapsed}" #expandArea>
  <button text-button (click)="expandArea.toggleExpand()" size="md" variant="ghost" symbolLeft="collapse_all">Collapse area</button>
</ExpandArea>
<!-- args:
{
    title: 'Expand Area Title',
    padding: 'sm',
    initCollapsed: true,
  }
-->

api.name: ariaLabel

api.name: ariaLabel
api.description: Screen-reader text for the badge (e.g. "5 unread messages"). Without it a sensible default is announced; a bare number or dot carries no meaning.

      { value: 'option1', label: 'Option 1' },
      { value: 'option2', label: 'Option 2' },
      { value: 'option3', label: 'Option 3' },
    ],
    value: 'option2',
    disabled: true,
    touched: false,
    invalid: false,
    dirty: false,
  }
-->

themes.name: Light Theme
themes.description: The Light Theme theme for the Uni Design System.
themes.usage: Apply style overrides through the Emotion theme provider (the `UniTheme` object), and pass component options as component inputs/props. Import the theme from `@uni-design-system/uni-core` (`LightTheme`).

themes.name: Dark Theme
themes.description: The Dark Theme theme for the Uni Design System.
themes.usage: Apply style overrides through the Emotion theme provider (the `UniTheme` object), and pass component options as component inputs/props. Import the theme from `@uni-design-system/uni-core` (`DarkTheme`).
